title: Curated Commons V1
objective: Connect maintainer plans and community proposals to one discoverable, claimable, resumable, and validated public work pipeline.
scope: Public work map, roadmap and milestone projection, Task Hall registry, Project discovery surface, and repository validation
non_goals: No package implementation, external write permission, automatic assignment, model ranking, or device-support claim

checkpoints.title: Publish the maintained public work map and planning contract
checkpoints.outcome: Human and machine readers can discover every registered live outcome, its origin, execution surface, authority, and exact next action without chat context.
non_goals: No package change or inference that appearing in a plan makes work Ready
do_not_touch: Repository permissions, secrets, releases, and device-support claims

required_capabilities: Can design public workbench information architecture and fail-closed JSON validation
required_tools: Git, Python 3, GitHub read access, and the repository validator
qualification_evidence: A validated positive map plus negative drift fixtures

acceptance.criterion: Maintainer plans and community proposals have separate entries and converge only at maintainer admission.
Every registered live outcome names its origin, family, priority, lifecycle, execution surfaces, milestone, umbrella, task contract, and exact next action.
The documentation assigns non-conflicting roles to Roadmap, milestones, Project, Issues, task contracts, registry, pull requests, and evidence.

verification.procedure: Validate the public work map against its schema and cross-check every initiative against the task registry.
verification.expected: The valid map passes and fixtures with missing, duplicate, or drifting task references fail.

verification.procedure: Review the public entry route from a fresh context.
verification.expected: A new contributor can select one bounded checkpoint without private chat or consumer context.

checkpoints.exact_next_action: Comment /claim CC-01 on issue #65, obtain maintainer confirmation, and publish a branch or draft pull-request anchor before material work.

checkpoints.title: Align GitHub surfaces, validate drift, and integrate
checkpoints.outcome: Roadmap, milestones, Project, umbrella and checkpoint Issues, registry, validation, and merge evidence agree at one reviewed commit.
non_goals: No automatic assignment, external permission grant, model scoring, or unverified device claim
do_not_touch: Repository secrets, external membership, force pushes, and device-support claims

required_capabilities: Can reconcile GitHub planning projections, authority boundaries, repository validation, and integration evidence
required_tools: Git, Python 3, GitHub Project and Issue access, and the repository validator
qualification_evidence: A reviewed field and lifecycle mapping, full validator output, public CI, and privacy scan

acceptance.criterion: Outcome milestones and Project fields expose origin, family, execution surface, priority, lifecycle, device gate, duty, contribution, and continuation.
Every planned public outcome has an umbrella, bounded checkpoint graph, task contract, registry entry, and integration owner.
Full repository validation, CI, privacy review, and maintainer merge decision pass.

verification.procedure: Inspect the live GitHub Project, milestones, parent-child Issues, registry, and public work map at the reviewed commit.
verification.expected: The live surfaces agree with machine authority and expose no private consumer or orchestration data.

verification.procedure: Run the full repository validator and Python contract suite, then reproduce CI on the public pull request.
verification.expected: All applicable checks pass at the reviewed commit.

checkpoints.exact_next_action: Wait for CC-01 completion, then request the integration lease and reconcile the live GitHub surfaces at the exact reviewed commit.

integration.strategy: The maintainer reviews the machine map, live GitHub projections, validation, privacy boundary, and public CI before merge.
